text: From my assessment this theory is better understood as soft panentheism, as I will explain and assess below, incorporating in particular Ward’s attempt to integrate scientific materialism into a theistic framework utilising a set of criteria appropriate to the category of panentheism referred to by the bipolar formula God in more than one sense. In the broader context of the reformational tradition the category of panentheism together with its soft variant is a trend in present-day theism to which one discerns not inconsiderable developments amongst some branches of Christian theology. Building on his presentation of God's 'divine realm', and employing the theory of "God-experience" instead of the doctrine of revelation according to traditional Protestant dogmatics, Ward turns the Christian dogmatic discipline on its head as he integrates within it his philosophical theistic process theory of God. An urgent word of warning however will be called for by anyone, tempted by this general Christian panentheism.
headline: Fully Human Written
